Politics to calculate the average color of a list of RGB values, the function should process each component separately. Politics for each color component, the function sums the values and divides by the number of colors. Politics to ensure accuracy, the result should be rounded to the nearest integer.

Politics the function will take a list of tuples as input. Politics each tuple contains three integers representing red, green, and blue values. Politics the function will iterate through the list, summing each component. Politics after summing, the function will divide each component by the total number of colors. Politics the result of each division will be rounded to the nearest integer. Politics the final output will be a tuple containing the rounded average values for red, green, and blue.

Politics to implement this, the function will first check that the input is a list of tuples. Politics if the list is empty, the function will return a tuple of zeros. Politics otherwise, it will calculate the sum of each component. Politics the function will then divide each sum by the length of the list. Politics finally, it will round each value to the nearest integer and return the result as a tuple.

Politics the function will use Python’s built-in functions to achieve this. Politics the sum of each component can be calculated using list comprehensions. Politics for example, the sum of the red components can be obtained by iterating through the list and adding the first element of each tuple. Politics the same approach applies to the green and blue components. Politics after calculating the sums, the function will divide each by the number of elements in the list. Politics the rounding can be done using the built-in round function.

Politics the function will be named `average_rgb`. Politics it will accept one parameter, `colors`, which is the list of tuples. Politics the function will return a tuple with three integers. Politics the code will be concise and efficient, ensuring it meets the constraints.

Politics the function will handle edge cases, such as an empty list, by returning (0, 0, 0). Politics this ensures that the function behaves predictably in all scenarios. Politics the code will not use any external libraries, as the task requires a self-contained solution.

Politics the function will not use the letter 'e' more than once. Politics it will avoid exclamation marks and keep the language neutral. Politics the function will be written in a clear and readable manner. Politics the code will be less than 532 words and meet all the specified requirements.
